enrichment.short_description: Clerk is a full-stack authentication and user management platform with prebuilt UI components for React, Next.js, and other frameworks.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Clerk offers prebuilt auth UI, B2B organizations, and billing for developers. Free up to 50,000 MAU; Pro plans start at $25/month.

features.title: Prebuilt UI components
features.description: Drop-in sign-up, sign-in, user profile, and waitlist components that can be embedded without building custom auth screens.

features.title: B2B organizations
features.description: Multi-tenant organization support with custom roles, auto-join, and invitations for B2B SaaS products.

features.title: Billing integration
features.description: Built-in subscription management and plan-based content gating tied directly to user accounts.

features.title: Security controls
features.description: Multi-factor authentication, bot detection, fraud detection, and managed session handling.

features.title: Multiple sign-on methods
features.description: Supports social sign-on, magic links, passwords, SMS, and API keys.

features.title: Broad framework support
features.description: SDKs for Next.js, React, React Router, TanStack React Start, Astro, and Expo for mobile.

pros: Generous free tier covering 50,000 monthly active users and 100 organizations with no credit card required
Prebuilt, customizable UI components reduce time spent building auth screens
First-class B2B multi-tenancy via the Organizations feature
Wide framework and platform support including mobile via Expo
cons: Enterprise SSO connections are billed separately at $75/month each on the Pro plan
Billing add-on charges 0.7% of processed billing volume on top of the base plan
Enterprise-tier features require a custom, annual-only contract

faqs.question: Does Clerk have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes. The Hobby plan is free for up to 50,000 monthly active users and 100 monthly active organizations, with no credit card required.

faqs.question: What frameworks does Clerk support?
faqs.answer: Clerk provides SDKs for Next.js, React, React Router, TanStack React Start, Astro, and Expo.

faqs.question: How much does Clerk cost after the free tier?
faqs.answer: The Pro plan starts at $25/month ($20/month billed annually), and the Business plan starts at $300/month ($250/month billed annually).

faqs.question: Does Clerk support B2B multi-tenancy?
faqs.answer: Yes, through its Organizations feature, which includes custom roles, auto-join, and invitations.

faqs.question: Is enterprise SSO available on Clerk?
faqs.answer: Yes, enterprise connections can be added to the Pro plan at $75/month each, or included via the Business and Enterprise plans.

use_cases.title: B2C SaaS authentication
use_cases.description: Add sign-up, sign-in, and session management to a consumer-facing application without building auth from scratch.

use_cases.title: B2B multi-tenant apps
use_cases.description: Manage organizations, roles, and invitations for products serving multiple companies from one codebase.

use_cases.title: Enterprise-ready applications
use_cases.description: Add SAML/SSO connections, SOC2 reporting, and priority support for larger customer deployments.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Clerk?
paragraphs: Clerk is a full-stack user management and authentication platform that provides prebuilt UI components for sign-up, sign-in, user profiles, and organization management.

Beyond core authentication, Clerk offers B2B multi-tenancy tools, subscription billing integration, and security features like MFA and bot detection, with SDKs for Next.js, React, and other popular frameworks.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Clerk is for
paragraphs: Clerk suits developers and teams building B2C or B2B web applications who want to avoid building authentication and user management infrastructure from scratch, from early-stage startups on the free tier through to enterprises needing SSO and compliance features.

enrichment.short_description: Logto is an open-source authentication platform offering passwordless login, enterprise SSO, RBAC, and multi-tenancy for SaaS and AI applications.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Logto provides open-source identity infrastructure with passwordless auth, enterprise SSO, and RBAC. Free up to 50,000 MAU; Pro starts at $24/month.

features.title: Passwordless and social sign-in
features.description: Email/SMS passwordless login alongside social sign-in and traditional password authentication.

features.title: Multi-factor authentication
features.description: WebAuthn, authenticator apps, and backup codes for additional login security.

features.title: Enterprise SSO
features.description: SAML and OIDC-based single sign-on for enterprise customers.

features.title: Role-based access control and multi-tenancy
features.description: Organizations feature supports multi-tenant apps with role-based permissions.

features.title: Machine-to-machine authentication
features.description: Supports service-to-service auth flows alongside user-facing sign-in.

features.title: Protected App (no-code)
features.description: A no-code option to add authentication in front of an existing app without SDK integration.

features.title: Open source and self-hostable
features.description: Full source is available, and Logto can be self-hosted instead of using the managed cloud.

pros: Free tier includes 50,000 monthly active users with no credit card required
Open-source with a self-hosting option for teams wanting full control over their identity infrastructure
Enterprise SSO (SAML/OIDC) is included on the $24/month Pro plan rather than gated behind a custom Enterprise tier
Supports 20+ frameworks including Next.js, React, Vue, Angular, Go, Swift, and Python
cons: Token usage beyond the included free allotment is billed per use ($0.08 per 100 extra tokens on Pro)
Pro plan audit log retention is limited to 14 days
Custom data regions, private cloud, and premium support require contacting sales for Enterprise pricing

faqs.question: Is Logto free to use?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Free plan supports up to 50,000 monthly active users with no credit card required.

faqs.question: Does Logto support enterprise SSO?
faqs.answer: Yes, SAML and OIDC-based enterprise SSO is included on the $24/month Pro plan.

faqs.question: Can Logto be self-hosted?
faqs.answer: Yes, Logto is open source and can be self-hosted instead of using the managed cloud service.

faqs.question: What is Logto's Pro plan price?
faqs.answer: The Pro plan costs $24/month and includes unlimited MAU, RBAC, organizations, MFA, and enterprise SSO.

faqs.question: Does Logto support multi-tenancy?
faqs.answer: Yes, through its Organizations feature, which is available on the Pro plan.

use_cases.title: SaaS multi-tenant authentication
use_cases.description: Add organizations, RBAC, and multi-tenant user management to a SaaS product.

use_cases.title: Enterprise SSO compliance
use_cases.description: Offer SAML/OIDC single sign-on to enterprise customers without building it in-house.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted identity infrastructure
use_cases.description: Run Logto on private infrastructure for full data control and compliance requirements.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Logto?
paragraphs: Logto is an open-source authentication and identity infrastructure platform aimed at simplifying identity management for SaaS and AI applications.
It offers passwordless login, social sign-in, MFA, enterprise SSO, role-based access control, and multi-tenancy, with SDKs for 20+ frameworks and the option to self-host.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Logto is for
paragraphs: Logto is suited to developers building SaaS or B2B products who need multi-tenancy and enterprise SSO, as well as teams that prefer an open-source, self-hostable identity platform over a fully closed managed service.

enrichment.short_description: Hanko is an open-source, passkey-first authentication platform offering cloud, private cloud, and self-hosted deployment options.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Hanko offers open-source, passkey-first authentication with a free 10,000 MAU tier and paid plans from $29/month.

features.title: Passkey-based authentication
features.description: FIDO2-certified passwordless login using passkeys as the primary sign-in method.

features.title: Passwords and passcodes
features.description: Traditional password and one-time passcode login options alongside passkeys.

features.title: Multi-factor authentication
features.description: MFA support via security keys and authenticator apps.

features.title: Social login
features.description: Sign-in with Google as a social login option.

features.title: Hanko Elements
features.description: Customizable Web Components for building auth UI without starting from scratch.

features.title: Flexible hosting
features.description: Available as managed cloud, managed private cloud, or fully self-hosted deployment.

pros: Free Starter plan includes 10,000 monthly active users and 2 projects at no cost
Passkey-first design reduces reliance on passwords
Deployment flexibility across cloud, private cloud, and self-hosted options
Startup program can offer up to one million free monthly active users for qualifying startups
cons: SAML SSO is a paid add-on at $49/month per connection, even on the Pro plan
Extended audit log retention and admin dashboard SSO require the custom-priced Enterprise plan
Community support is the only support option on the free Starter plan

faqs.question: Does Hanko have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Starter plan is free and includes 10,000 monthly active users and 2 projects.

faqs.question: What is Hanko's main authentication method?
faqs.answer: Hanko is built around FIDO2-certified passkey authentication, alongside passwords, passcodes, and social login.

faqs.question: Can Hanko be self-hosted?
faqs.answer: Yes, Hanko supports self-hosted deployment in addition to managed cloud and private cloud options.

faqs.question: How much does Hanko's Pro plan cost?
faqs.answer: The Pro plan costs $29/month plus $0.01 per monthly active user over 10,000.

faqs.question: Does Hanko offer a startup discount?
faqs.answer: Yes, qualifying startups can get up to one million monthly active users for free under Hanko's startup offering.

use_cases.title: Passwordless consumer apps
use_cases.description: Replace password login with passkeys to reduce account takeover risk and login friction.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted authentication
use_cases.description: Run Hanko on private infrastructure for full control over user data and compliance.

use_cases.title: Startup authentication at scale
use_cases.description: Use the startup program to get generous free MAU while scaling a new product.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Hanko?
paragraphs: Hanko is an open-source authentication and user management platform built around passkey login, positioned as an alternative to services like Auth0, Clerk, Firebase Auth, and Keycloak.
It supports passwords, passcodes, social login, and MFA alongside passkeys, with customizable UI components and cloud, private cloud, or self-hosted deployment options.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Hanko is for
paragraphs: Hanko fits developers who want a passkey-first, open-source authentication option, particularly teams that value deployment flexibility or want to self-host their identity infrastructure.

enrichment.short_description: Corbado is a passkey intelligence platform that gives visibility into login journeys and helps drive passkey adoption alongside existing identity providers.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Corbado adds passkey intelligence and login journey observability on top of existing identity providers like Okta, Auth0, and Cognito.

features.title: Journey reconstruction
features.description: Turns scattered client-side login events into a single trusted journey model showing branches, retries, fallbacks, and outcomes.

features.title: Friction detection
features.description: Identifies where users abandon login flows so teams can address drop-off points.

features.title: Per-user debugging
features.description: Lets teams inspect specific failed authentication attempts at the individual user level.

features.title: Passkey intelligence controls
features.description: Manages passkey rollout and routes users through the appropriate authentication path.

features.title: Authentication analytics
features.description: Tracks adoption rates, recovery load, and completion metrics across authentication methods.

features.title: Non-invasive IDP compatibility
features.description: Works alongside existing identity providers such as Okta, Auth0, Ping Identity, Cognito, and Entra without replacing them.

pros: Operates as a non-invasive layer on top of existing identity providers rather than requiring migration
Compatible with 20+ CIAM systems including Okta, Auth0, Ping Identity, ForgeRock, Cognito, and Entra
Certified for ISO 27001, SOC 2 Type II, and GDPR compliance
Integrates with monitoring and analytics tools such as Splunk, Datadog, New Relic, Amplitude, and Mixpanel
cons: Pricing is not published; both Observe and Connect plans require contacting sales
Reported adoption metrics (e.g., mobile activation gains) come from specific case studies and may not generalize to every deployment
Focused specifically on passkey/login analytics rather than being a full identity provider replacement

faqs.question: What does Corbado do?
faqs.answer: Corbado provides visibility into login journeys, helping teams detect friction and increase passkey adoption alongside their existing identity provider.

faqs.question: Does Corbado replace my identity provider?
faqs.answer: No, Corbado's Observe product works as a non-invasive layer on top of existing IDPs such as Okta, Auth0, and Cognito; Connect offers managed passkeys.

faqs.question: How much does Corbado cost?
faqs.answer: Corbado does not publish fixed pricing; both the Observe and Connect plans require contacting the company for a custom quote.

faqs.question: Is Corbado compliant with security standards?
faqs.answer: Yes, Corbado states it is ISO 27001, SOC 2 Type II, and GDPR compliant.

use_cases.title: Passkey rollout analytics
use_cases.description: Track passkey adoption and recovery load while rolling out passwordless login.

use_cases.title: Checkout conversion optimization
use_cases.description: Reduce login friction that causes drop-off during checkout or sign-in flows.

use_cases.title: Managed passkey deployment
use_cases.description: Use Corbado Connect to add managed passkey sign-up and sign-in without building the infrastructure in-house.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Corbado?
paragraphs: Corbado is an authentication intelligence platform that reconstructs login journeys to show where users encounter friction or abandon sign-in flows.

It offers two products: Corbado Observe, a read-only observability layer, and Corbado Connect, which adds managed passkey sign-up and sign-in, both designed to work alongside existing identity providers.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Corbado is for
paragraphs: Corbado is aimed at identity/IAM teams, product managers, and security or support teams at organizations that already use an identity provider but want deeper visibility into login friction and passkey adoption.

enrichment.short_description: Frontegg is a customer identity and access management platform offering hosted login, enterprise SSO, and usage-based pricing.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Frontegg provides low-code customer identity management with hosted login and enterprise SSO, with a free tier for up to 7,500 MAU.

features.title: Hosted login
features.description: Fully customized hosted login pages that can be embedded without building auth screens from scratch.

features.title: Enterprise SSO/SCIM connections
features.description: Support for SSO and SCIM connections aimed at enterprise customer requirements.

features.title: Multi-tenant organizations
features.description: Unlimited organizations supported for B2B SaaS multi-tenancy.

features.title: Usage-based pricing calculator
features.description: Interactive calculator that prices plans based on monthly active users, SSO connections, and M2M tokens.

features.title: Custom domain support
features.description: Ability to serve the hosted login experience from a custom domain.

features.title: Agentic access layer (Agen.co)
features.description: A related offering enabling users to interact with SaaS products through generative AI platforms.

pros: Free Pay-as-you-Go tier includes 7,500 monthly active users and 5 enterprise SSO/SCIM connections
Custom domain and fully customized hosted login included even on the free tier
Usage-based calculator lets teams estimate costs as usage scales
Enterprise tier adds advanced fraud protection and a 99.99% uptime SLA
cons: Specific per-unit pricing beyond the free tier is not published; requires using the calculator or contacting sales
Enterprise features such as advanced fraud protection and premium support require a custom-priced contract
Marketing site provides limited detail on specific third-party integrations

faqs.question: Does Frontegg have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Pay-as-you-Go plan is free and includes 7,500 monthly active users and 5 enterprise SSO/SCIM connections.

faqs.question: How is Frontegg priced beyond the free tier?
faqs.answer: Frontegg uses a usage-based calculator based on monthly active users, enterprise connections, and M2M tokens; exact per-unit rates require contacting sales.

faqs.question: Does Frontegg support enterprise SSO?
faqs.answer: Yes, enterprise SSO/SCIM connections are included starting on the free Pay-as-you-Go plan, with more available on Enterprise.

faqs.question: What is Agen.co?
faqs.answer: Agen.co is Frontegg's related offering that lets users interact with SaaS products through generative AI platforms.

use_cases.title: SaaS customer identity management
use_cases.description: Add hosted login, organizations, and SSO to a SaaS product without building identity infrastructure in-house.

use_cases.title: Enterprise customer onboarding
use_cases.description: Offer SSO/SCIM connections and advanced fraud protection to satisfy enterprise procurement requirements.
use_cases.audience: B2B software vendors selling to enterprises

features: Everything in Pay as You Go

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Frontegg?
paragraphs: Frontegg is a customer identity and access management platform that describes itself as the identity layer for every SaaS entry point, offering hosted login, enterprise SSO, and multi-tenant organization support.
Pricing is based on a usage calculator covering monthly active users, enterprise SSO/SCIM connections, and machine-to-machine tokens, with a free tier and a custom-priced Enterprise tier.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Frontegg is for
paragraphs: Frontegg is aimed at SaaS companies that want to implement customer identity and enterprise SSO without building and maintaining the infrastructure themselves.

enrichment.short_description: SlashID is an identity security platform that detects and remediates identity-based threats across human, machine, and AI identities.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: SlashID detects identity-based threats across human, machine, and AI identities with 500+ built-in detections mapped to MITRE ATT&CK.

features.title: Identity threat detection
features.description: 500+ built-in detections mapped to the MITRE ATT&CK framework.

features.title: Risk and posture management
features.description: Continuous scoring of identities across exposure and entitlements.

features.title: Access reviews and governance
features.description: Automated certification campaigns and compliance workflows.

features.title: Browser extension
features.description: Edge-level phishing detection, shadow SaaS discovery, and session hijacking prevention.

features.title: Remediation automation
features.description: One-click enforcement of MFA, user suspension, or credential rotation.

features.title: Broad integrations
features.description: 100+ integrations across cloud providers, SaaS apps, databases, and identity providers.

pros: Covers human, machine, and AI identities under one system of record
500+ built-in detections mapped to the MITRE ATT&CK framework
Sub-1-second detection-to-remediation capability claimed
SOC 2 Type II certified and supports compliance workflows for SOC 2, ISO 27001, PCI DSS, HIPAA, and GDPR
cons: No public pricing is available; the site directs visitors to request a demo
Aimed at organizations with hybrid/multi-cloud complexity, which may be more than smaller teams need
Specific implementation and onboarding details are not published on the marketing site

faqs.question: What does SlashID do?
faqs.answer: SlashID detects and helps remediate identity-based breach attacks across human, machine, and AI identities.

faqs.question: How many integrations does SlashID support?
faqs.answer: SlashID states it supports 100+ integrations across cloud providers, SaaS applications, databases, and identity providers.

faqs.question: Is SlashID pricing public?
faqs.answer: No, SlashID does not publish pricing; the site directs visitors to request a demo.

faqs.question: What compliance standards does SlashID support?
faqs.answer: SlashID is SOC 2 Type II certified and supports compliance workflows for SOC 2, ISO 27001, PCI DSS, HIPAA, and GDPR.

use_cases.title: Identity threat detection
use_cases.description: Detect account takeover, credential misuse, and identity-based attacks across an organization.

use_cases.title: Access governance and compliance
use_cases.description: Run automated access review campaigns to satisfy compliance requirements.

use_cases.title: Multi-cloud identity risk management
use_cases.description: Score and manage identity risk and entitlements across hybrid and multi-cloud environments.
use_cases.audience: Security leaders at mid-size to large organizations

long_form_blocks.heading: What is SlashID?
paragraphs: SlashID is an identity security platform positioned as one system of record for human, machine, and AI identities, offering detection, governance, and audit capabilities.
It includes 500+ built-in threat detections mapped to MITRE ATT&CK, risk and posture scoring, automated access reviews, and a browser extension for phishing and session-hijacking detection.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who SlashID is for
paragraphs: SlashID targets security leaders and teams managing identity risk across hybrid and multi-cloud environments that need to meet compliance requirements such as SOC 2, ISO 27001, PCI DSS, HIPAA, or GDPR.

enrichment.short_description: Scalekit provides authentication and authorization for AI agents, handling delegated OAuth, token management, and SaaS auth from one platform.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Scalekit handles delegated OAuth and auth for AI agents with 500+ connectors, plus SaaS SSO and RBAC. Free tier, then $99/month.

features.title: Agentkit delegated OAuth
features.description: Handles delegated OAuth flows and token management with an AES-256 encrypted token vault so agents can act on behalf of users.

features.title: 500+ pre-built connectors
features.description: Ready-made connectors across productivity, CRM, project management, and data tools.

features.title: Scoped per-tool-call permissions
features.description: Permissions are scoped per tool call rather than per agent, with full attribution logging for every action.

features.title: Auth for SaaS
features.description: User and organization management, RBAC, and SSO with SAML/OIDC support for SaaS applications.

features.title: Auth for MCP
features.description: OAuth 2.1 support for Model Context Protocol servers.

features.title: Agent Gateway (early access)
features.description: A deployment control plane for managing internal agent teams and connector catalogs.

features.title: Native AI framework integrations
features.description: Integrations with Anthropic Claude, OpenAI, LangChain, Google ADK, Vercel AI, and Mastra.

pros: Free tier includes 5,000 tool calls per month with unlimited connected accounts
500+ pre-built connectors reduce the work of integrating agents with common SaaS tools
Native integrations with major AI agent frameworks including Claude, OpenAI, and LangChain
SOC 2 and ISO 27001 certified with multi-region (US/EU) deployment
cons: Free plan auto-upgrades to the paid Growth tier after exceeding 5,000 monthly tool calls
EU data residency is a paid add-on (+$99/month) on the Growth plan
VPC/on-premises deployment and forward-deployed engineer support require custom Enterprise pricing

faqs.question: Does Scalekit have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Free plan includes 5,000 tool calls per month with unlimited connected accounts and 500+ pre-built connectors.

faqs.question: How much does Scalekit's Growth plan cost?
faqs.answer: The Growth plan costs $99/month and includes 100,000 tool calls per month, with $0.50 per 1,000 in overage.

faqs.question: What happens if I exceed the free tool call limit?
faqs.answer: The Free plan auto-upgrades to the paid Growth plan after exceeding 5,000 monthly tool calls.

faqs.question: Does Scalekit support AI agent frameworks?
faqs.answer: Yes, Scalekit offers native integrations with Anthropic Claude, OpenAI, LangChain, Google ADK, Vercel AI, and Mastra.

faqs.question: Where is Scalekit based?
faqs.answer: Scalekit is based in San Francisco, CA.

use_cases.title: AI agent tool access
use_cases.description: Let AI agents securely act on behalf of users across 500+ connected tools using delegated OAuth.

use_cases.title: SaaS authentication and SSO
use_cases.description: Add user management, RBAC, and SAML/OIDC SSO to a SaaS product.

use_cases.title: Internal agent deployment governance
use_cases.description: Manage and approve connector catalogs for internal AI agent teams via the Agent Gateway.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Scalekit?
paragraphs: Scalekit is an authentication and authorization platform built specifically for AI agents, handling OAuth flows, token management, and credential storage so agents can act on behalf of users without shared service accounts.
Alongside Agentkit for agent authentication, Scalekit also offers Auth for SaaS (user management, RBAC, SSO) and Auth for MCP (OAuth 2.1 for Model Context Protocol servers).

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Scalekit is for
paragraphs: Scalekit is built for AI agent developers, SaaS companies adding agent features, and enterprises deploying internal AI agents that need secure, scoped access to external tools and systems.

enrichment.short_description: PropelAuth is a B2B authentication platform providing multi-tenant organizations, RBAC, and enterprise SSO with prebuilt UI components.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: PropelAuth offers B2B authentication with organizations, RBAC, and enterprise SSO. Free up to 10,000 MAU; Growth plan starts at $150/month.

features.title: Prebuilt UI components
features.description: Customizable component library for rapid deployment of auth screens.

features.title: Organization management
features.description: First-class support for organizations, built for multi-tenant B2B products.

features.title: Role-based access control
features.description: Custom roles and permissions for managing what users can access.

features.title: Enterprise SSO
features.description: Self-service SAML, OIDC, and SCIM setup for enterprise customers.

features.title: MCP server authentication
features.description: OAuth 2.1 flows and token revocation for Model Context Protocol servers.

features.title: User management dashboard
features.description: Includes impersonation capabilities and audit logs.

features.title: API key authentication
features.description: API key issuance with rate limiting for machine access.

pros: Free plan includes 10,000 monthly active users with unlimited organizations
Self-service SAML/SSO setup reduces integration overhead for enterprise customers
Supports MCP authentication for AI-agent use cases
Broad framework and language support including React, Next.js, FastAPI, Flask, .NET, Spring Boot, and Go
cons: SCIM/directory sync requires the $500/month Growth Plus plan
Additional charges apply for extra MAUs ($0.05/user), SCIM connections ($100 each), and audit log export ($50/connection)
Advanced security options are gated behind the higher-priced Growth Plus tier

faqs.question: Does PropelAuth have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Free plan includes 10,000 monthly active users, unlimited organizations, 2FA, and RBAC.

faqs.question: How much does PropelAuth's Growth plan cost?
faqs.answer: The Growth plan costs $150/month and adds user impersonation, unlimited SAML/enterprise SSO, and MCP authentication.

faqs.question: Does PropelAuth support SCIM?
faqs.answer: Yes, SCIM/directory sync is available on the $500/month Growth Plus plan, with additional per-connection charges.

faqs.question: Can PropelAuth authenticate MCP servers?
faqs.answer: Yes, PropelAuth supports MCP server authentication on its Growth plan and above.

faqs.question: What frameworks does PropelAuth support?
faqs.answer: PropelAuth supports React, Next.js, FastAPI, Flask, .NET, Spring Boot, Remix, Axum, Go, Laravel, and others.

use_cases.title: B2B SaaS authentication
use_cases.description: Add multi-tenant organizations and role-based access control to a B2B product.

use_cases.title: Enterprise SSO and SCIM onboarding
use_cases.description: Offer self-service SAML/SSO and SCIM directory sync to satisfy enterprise procurement requirements.
use_cases.audience: Scaling B2B companies selling to enterprises

use_cases.title: AI-native application auth
use_cases.description: Authenticate MCP servers and AI-native products using OAuth 2.1.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is PropelAuth?
paragraphs: PropelAuth is a B2B authentication platform built around multi-tenant organizations, offering prebuilt UI components, role-based access control, and self-service enterprise SSO/SCIM setup.
It also supports MCP server authentication via OAuth 2.1, positioning itself for AI-native companies alongside traditional B2B SaaS products.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who PropelAuth is for
paragraphs: PropelAuth is aimed at B2B SaaS companies at any growth stage, from pre-launch startups using the free tier to scaling companies that need enterprise SSO, SCIM, and advanced organization controls.

enrichment.short_description: Casdoor is an open-source identity and access management platform supporting OAuth, OIDC, SAML, LDAP, and an MCP server for AI-agent management.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Casdoor is an open-source IAM and SSO platform with OAuth, OIDC, SAML, LDAP support, and a built-in MCP server for AI-agent management.

features.title: Multi-protocol IAM
features.description: Supports OAuth 2.0, OIDC, SAML, CAS, LDAP, WebAuthn, and MFA.

features.title: 100+ identity provider integrations
features.description: Built-in connectors for social networks and enterprise identity systems such as Google, GitHub, and WeChat.

features.title: MCP server
features.description: A built-in Model Context Protocol server that lets AI agents manage Casdoor through natural language.

features.title: OAuth 2.1 with Dynamic Client Registration
features.description: Includes per-tool permissions designed for agent-based access.

features.title: Built-in SaaS billing
features.description: Plans, pricing tiers, payment processing, and subscription management features.

features.title: OpenClaw integration
features.description: LLM agent protection with audit trails via OpenTelemetry.

features.title: Open source and self-hostable
features.description: Fully open-source project that can be self-hosted.

pros: Fully open source and self-hostable at no license cost
Very broad protocol support including OAuth2, OIDC, SAML, CAS, LDAP, and WebAuthn
100+ built-in identity provider connectors
Native MCP server lets AI coding assistants like Claude Desktop, Cursor, and VS Code Copilot manage Casdoor via natural language
Used by large organizations including Intel, VMware Harbor, Docker, and Cisco
cons: No published pricing or SLA for any managed/commercial offering; self-hosting requires operational effort
Documentation and setup are aimed at a technical/developer audience
As a broad multi-protocol platform, initial configuration may require more setup than single-purpose auth tools

faqs.question: Is Casdoor free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Casdoor is an open-source project and is free to self-host.

faqs.question: What protocols does Casdoor support?
faqs.answer: Casdoor supports OAuth 2.0, OIDC, SAML, CAS, LDAP, WebAuthn, and MFA.

faqs.question: Does Casdoor support AI agent management?
faqs.answer: Yes, Casdoor includes a built-in MCP server that lets AI agents like Claude Desktop, Cursor, and VS Code Copilot manage it through natural language.

faqs.question: Who uses Casdoor?
faqs.answer: Casdoor lists notable users including Intel, VMware Harbor, Docker, Cisco, Microsoft, Alibaba, and Tencent.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted enterprise IAM
use_cases.description: Run a full-featured identity and SSO platform on private infrastructure.

use_cases.title: AI-agent-manageable authentication
use_cases.description: Let AI coding assistants configure and manage identity settings via the built-in MCP server.
use_cases.audience: Developer teams using AI coding tools

use_cases.title: Multi-protocol SSO
use_cases.description: Consolidate OAuth, OIDC, SAML, CAS, and LDAP-based sign-in under one platform.
use_cases.audience: Organizations with mixed legacy and modern identity systems

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Casdoor?
paragraphs: Casdoor is an open-source identity and access management (IAM) and single sign-on (SSO) platform supporting OAuth 2.0, OIDC, SAML, CAS, LDAP, WebAuthn, and MFA, along with 100+ identity provider integrations.

It includes a built-in MCP server that lets AI agents and coding assistants such as Claude Desktop, Cursor, and VS Code with GitHub Copilot manage Casdoor configuration through natural language, plus OpenClaw integration for LLM agent protection.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Casdoor is for
paragraphs: Casdoor is built for enterprise teams, SaaS platforms, and startups that want a self-hosted, open-source identity platform with broad protocol support and AI-agent manageability.

enrichment.short_description: OpenSign is an open-source PDF e-signature platform offering unlimited free signatures with an option to self-host.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: OpenSign is an open-source e-signature tool with unlimited free signing, document vault storage, and self-hosting options.

features.title: Unlimited free e-signatures
features.description: Sign documents with no cap on the number of signatures on the free plan.

features.title: Multi-user collaboration
features.description: Multiple users can collaborate on the same documents.

features.title: Completion certificates
features.description: Signed documents include completion certificates with access logs.

features.title: OpenSign Drive
features.description: A secure vault for storing and organizing signed and pending documents.

features.title: Self-hosting option
features.description: OpenSign can be self-hosted instead of using the hosted app.opensignlabs.com service.

features.title: API access
features.description: API available for integrating e-signature workflows into other applications.

pros: Free plan offers unlimited e-signatures with no cost
Open-source alternative to DocuSign, with source code available on GitHub
Self-hosting option gives organizations full control over document data
Completion certificates with access logs support audit and compliance needs
cons: Specific paid-tier pricing was not visible on the public pricing page at time of review
Community support is primarily via Discord rather than dedicated account management
As a newer open-source tool, its third-party integration ecosystem may be smaller than established competitors like DocuSign

faqs.question: Is OpenSign free?
faqs.answer: Yes, OpenSign offers a free plan with unlimited e-signatures.

faqs.question: Can OpenSign be self-hosted?
faqs.answer: Yes, OpenSign is open source and can be self-hosted instead of using the hosted app.opensignlabs.com service.

faqs.question: Does OpenSign provide proof of signing?
faqs.answer: Yes, signed documents include completion certificates with access logs.

faqs.question: Is there an API for OpenSign?
faqs.answer: Yes, OpenSign provides API access for integrating e-signature workflows into other applications.

use_cases.title: Free document signing
use_cases.description: Collect e-signatures on contracts and forms without a per-signature cost.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted e-signature compliance
use_cases.description: Host the e-signature platform on private infrastructure to keep document data in-house.
use_cases.audience: Organizations with data residency or compliance requirements

use_cases.title: Embedded e-signature workflows
use_cases.description: Use the API to add e-signature capability inside another application.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is OpenSign?
paragraphs: OpenSign is an open-source PDF e-signature platform positioned as a free alternative to DocuSign, offering unlimited signatures on its free plan.
It includes multi-user collaboration, completion certificates with access logs, and OpenSign Drive for secure document storage, with both a hosted app and a self-hosting option.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who OpenSign is for
paragraphs: OpenSign suits individuals and businesses looking for a free or self-hosted alternative to commercial e-signature tools, especially those who value open-source software and control over document data.
